advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Align the test-file comment's canonicalization order with the actual code order (NFC -> relativize -> normpath).
evidence: tests/unit/test_code_review_ws5.py:427: `now canonicalized (relativize-under-repo_root -> normpath -> NFC) before the membership test.`
detectors.py:41-49: `s = unicodedata.normalize("NFC", str(p))` then `rel = os.path.relpath(...)` then `return os.path.normpath(s)` — actual order is NFC, relativize, normpath
advisory.finding: The test comment documents the canonicalization order as 'relativize-under-repo_root -> normpath -> NFC', but the implementation applies NFC first, then relativize, then normpath. The stated order contradicts the code.
scenarios: A future maintainer reads the comment to reason about ordering-sensitive edge cases (e.g., whether normpath runs before or after NFC) and is misled.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test asserting an absolute location.file OUTSIDE repo_root does not match a same-tail relative changed-file entry (exercises the `..` guard at detectors.py:45).
evidence: src/rebar/llm/code_review/detectors.py:45: `if not rel.startswith(".."):  # only relativize when genuinely under repo_root`

The negative test uses a RELATIVE path: tests/unit/test_code_review_ws5.py:479 `location_file="other.py"` — it never triggers the `os.path.isabs(s)` branch, so the under-repo_root guard is unexercised.
ABSENCE: no test passes an absolute location_file under a DIFFERENT root (e.g. `/other/tree/app.py` with repo_root=tmp_path) to assert it does not match a changed `app.py`.
advisory.finding: No test exercises the `_canonical_path` branch where an absolute `location.file` lies OUTSIDE repo_root (the `if not rel.startswith("..")` guard, line 45). This distinct new behavior path — an out-of-tree absolute match path must NOT be relativized into a spurious diff-scope match — is untested; a regression that dropped the `..` guard (relativizing all absolute paths) would go undetected.
scenarios: A detector reports an absolute location.file for a file outside the review's repo_root while changed_files contains a same-tail relative entry (e.g. app.py).
A future edit removes or inverts the `not rel.startswith('..')` guard.
advisory.suggested_fix: Add: `assert not _security_match_lands(monkeypatch, location_file=str(tmp_path.parent / "elsewhere" / "app.py"), changed_files=["app.py"], repo_root=str(tmp_path))` to cover the out-of-tree absolute-path branch.

coaching.coaching: Update the user/operator/API docs that track the canonicalization order in the test comment.
